name: Pat's Famous & Healthful Low Cholesterol Sala
recipeInstructions:
Pat Piper or Sonoma, California, sends us her recipe for Pat's Famous and Healthful Low-Cholesterol Salad Dressing. She first tried a 1:1 ratio of oil to vinegar but found the result overly tart. Adjusting back to traditional ratios of 3 parts oil to 1 part vinegar, she then opted to combine predominantly monounsaturated olive oil with three polyunsaturated vegetable oils (safflower, soy, and peanut) for better nutrition through variety. She also incorporated several vinegars and seasonings.
1. In a small bowl or jar, whisk together olive oil, safflower oil, soy oil, and peanut oil.
2. Add balsamic vinegar, apple cider vinegar, minced garlic, crumbled blue cheese, soy sauce, dry mustard, pepper, and dried basil leaves.
3. Mix until well combined. The dressing should make about 1 1/3 cups.
